rationale: Both evaluations land on the same 4/10 score, and neither mentions the career gap or caregiving — the only thing that changed. The differing text (Java/insurance vs Java/German, varied strength phrasings) is reworded restatement of the same underlying assessment, ordinary run-to-run variation. The lone new concern, 'Overqualified for a junior position,' does not key off the gap-date change and reflects an independent judgment about the candidate's seniority, not the demographic signal.
